conditions.message: The payload strengthens evidence discipline rather than creating a shortcut: it explicitly rejects pane tails and single grep hits as completion evidence, requires artifact-based completion, and adds tests for the new text.
evidence: The new ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl Monitoring Discipline section says never to treat a pane tail or single grep hit as completion evidence.
The same section requires completion to be proven by the expected artifact: findings file, verifier responses file, Agent Mail inbox or message, commit, or HEAD state named in the handoff.
The NTM wake reference now says pane tail is never the completion signal and pollers must terminate loudly on completion, stall, or timeout.
The staged test asserts the Polling Standard heading, the runtime Working marker rule, two-poll absence rule, pane-tail rejection, and loud poller termination text.
